feedback.text: I cannot believe that video game publishers are seriously allowed to stop and de facto destroy a game for which people have spent real money. It is relatively extremely easy for a company that is putting millions in the development of a game to take simple measures to ensure that the game is still playable in some way even if the company no longer wants to keep the servers running. This is perfectly understandable and, of course, no one can be forced to bear the cost of doing so everywhere. But this does not require anyone to do so. It is simply a matter for firms to decide at any time that no one can play the game again, for which they have spent their hard-earned money. As citizens, we should not be subject to the arbitrary decisions of a group of companies, which is only about profit. If something is sold, one also bears the responsibility not to deliberately destroy this property, especially if it would have been possible, in theory, to maintain the game for everyone who bought it with minimal effort. That is why I would like this to be included in the Digital Fairness Act. Because what is more unfair than losing his possession through arbitrariness of another?
